meta.notes: Phase 1 proposed assumptions to confirm or override. | Phase 2: confirmed ASM-001/002; edited ASM-006 with demo/public-deploy caveat.

assumptions.assumption: The app will be implemented as a Lovable-hosted web app (Web + Desktop) with optional Mobile PWA support.
assumptions.why_needed: Templates require a concrete platform target; many UI and NFR budgets depend on platform.
assumptions.impact: Medium. If platform changes (e.g., Telegram mini-app), UI and hosting constraints change.

evidence.snippet: Review the attachments to create brd/src pack as defined in the brd_srs_packs.zip and to improve the GUT app prompt for lovable to create this application. The created brd/src foundation documentation

assumptions.assumption: Default deployment is 'kiosk/presenter mode' (no user login), with optional admin-only controls for uploading/replacing datasets.
assumptions.why_needed: Science fair demos usually require immediate access without credentials; protects from accidental edits.

evidence.snippet: Review the attachments to create brd/src pack as defined in the brd_srs_packs.zip and to improve the GUT app prompt for lovable to create this application. The created brd/src foundation documentation

assumptions.assumption: All simulation outputs are deterministic for the same (inputs + model_version), and each run records a run_hash for reproducibility.
assumptions.why_needed: Judge trust and scientific rigor; supports export reproducibility.
assumptions.impact: High on credibility, low on scope.

evidence.snippet: This approach allows for simulation of how changes in diet propagate through biological systems  and influence cognitive outcomes.

assumptions.assumption: No personally identifiable information is stored. Pilot dataset remains de-identified numeric metrics only.
assumptions.why_needed: Privacy posture for a teen dataset, and to avoid human-subjects compliance issues.

evidence.snippet: ○ De-identified 2025 student dataset containing only numeric diet quality scores and  cognitive performance metrics, used solely for validation and benchmarking and  not for model training
not for model training  Procedure  1. Download dietary intake data from NHANES and extract nutrient intake variables
File contains 66 rows of pilot data with diet score and cognitive tests (Stroop, Language, Memory, Logical, Overall).

assumptions.assumption: The app includes a visible disclaimer: educational research prototype; not medical advice; not a diagnostic tool.
assumptions.why_needed: Avoids medical/diagnostic overreach during judging and demos.
assumptions.impact: Low on cost, high on risk reduction.

evidence.snippet: **App fix:** add an “Educational research prototype” label + a small disclaimer: *not medical advice; not a diagnostic device.* This is consistent with how consumer microbiome companies themselves pre

assumptions.assumption: The pilot dataset file provided (Data Sheet 1.csv) is allowed to be embedded in the app for demo purposes.
assumptions.why_needed: If embedding is not allowed, app must upload at runtime, affecting hosting and demo flow.

evidence.snippet: File contains 66 rows of pilot data with diet score and cognitive tests (Stroop, Language, Memory, Logical, Overall).

assumptions.user_override: Bundle the de-identified pilot CSV for the offline judging build, but also support upload-at-runtime and include a note to remove bundled data for any public deployment.
